scope: Paid (Google Ads) visits only. US visitors. Internal/test visits excluded.
method: Based on PostHog session recordings for paid (Google Ads) visitors: each visit's start time, landing page, clicks, form activity and outcome. Anything unusual (such as an odd click pattern) is flagged for review and confirmed before being reported as an issue, rather than assumed.

posthog.filter: Paid Search channel, US, test visits excluded

reconciliation.note: Google Ads logged 5 paid clicks on June 18; PostHog captured 3 paid US visits as recordings, a difference of 2. A small gap between paid clicks and recordings is normal (very short or pre-load exits, or visitors whose session was not recorded). June 16 and June 17 paid-click counts, which were not yet posted on earlier runs, are now available (5 and 4) and have been filled into the rolling series below.

sessions.label: Visit 1
sessions.verdict: Came in from a Google ad to the Madison residential roofing page on an iPhone, browsed for about five and a half minutes without clicking or typing, then left. The longest paid visit of the day, but with no engagement and no form activity.

sessions.label: Visit 2
sessions.verdict: Arrived from a Google ad to the Madison residential roofing page on an Android phone, spent about three and a half minutes on the page without clicking or typing, then left (a browse-and-leave visit).

sessions.label: Visit 3
sessions.verdict: Came in from a Google ad to the Madison residential roofing page on a desktop computer and left after about nine seconds (a quick bounce).

insights.headline: 3 recorded paid visits on June 18, all browse-and-leave
insights.body: PostHog captured three paid US visits from Google Ads on June 18 - two on mobile phones and one on a desktop computer - all landing on the Madison residential roofing page. None of them clicked or typed; each browsed briefly and left.

insights.headline: The longest visit still didn't engage
insights.body: The day's longest paid visit stayed on the roofing page for about five and a half minutes on an iPhone, but never clicked or started the form. The other two were shorter, including a nine-second desktop bounce.

insights.headline: No form activity and nothing flagged
insights.body: There were no form starts, submissions, phone taps or confirmed friction on June 18, and no visit needed flagging for a closer look.

insights.headline: 5 paid clicks, 3 recorded visits
insights.body: Google Ads logged five paid clicks for June 18 and three were captured as recordings. A small gap like this is normal - some clicks end as very short or pre-load exits, or come from visitors whose session was not recorded. June 16 and June 17 paid-click counts have now been posted (5 and 4) and reconciled in the weekly view.

data_notes: Window: June 18, 2026 (US Central). Each visit is dated by its exact start time in US Central time.
Scope: paid (Google Ads) visits only, US visitors, internal and test visits excluded.
Privacy: visitor names and emails are not shown. Visits are referenced by anonymous labels and recording links only.

Paid clicks for June 18: 5, from the conversion dashboard's Google Ads daily series. June 16 (5) and June 17 (4), which were not yet posted on earlier runs, are now available and have been filled into the rolling series.
All three recorded visits browsed without form typing; there were no form starts, submissions, phone taps or confirmed friction on June 18.
Weekly totals (June 12-18): 29 paid clicks, 16 recorded visits, 1 form start, 0 submissions; recording capture rate about 55%.
headline_state: Paid visitor sessions for June 18 (US Central). 5 paid clicks and 3 recorded visits - two on mobile phones, one on desktop - all landing on the Madison residential roofing page. Every visit browsed briefly and left with no clicks or form typing. No form starts, submissions or phone taps, and nothing flagged for review.
